problem: Shared Redis is now a reliability and compliance bottleneck — where do we cache instead?
decision: Each service owns an in-process LRU plus an optional per-service Redis; there is no shared cache plane.
alternatives: Keep shared Redis, add per-region replicas
Move all caching to CDN edge
constraints: No service reads another service's cache directly
Cache key format documents the owning service
failures: Subtle coupling where one service implicitly relies on another's cached data
expiry_conditions: We reintroduce a shared materialized-view layer

future_reminders.who_might_repeat: engineer tempted to add a shared cache utility
future_reminders.revisit_when: cross-service read-through becomes common enough to justify a shared layer
